improvements_list: New INV-020 (Verification Prompt Coverage): formalizes the requirement that every element specification chapter includes a verification prompt block with positive and negative checks — closes a documented self-bootstrapping gap where v1 prescribed verification prompts at §5.6 but never required them in its own element spec chapters

New ADR-011 (ADR Supersession Protocol): locks the mark-and-supersede procedure for handling obsolete ADRs (mark old with SUPERSEDED status, create new ADR with fresh ID, execute cross-reference cascade via §13.3) — resolves Open Question #3 about spec evolution in living documents

Verification prompt blocks added to all 6 element specification chapters (Chapters 2–7): each block includes positive invariant checks, negative checks, and integration checks referencing specific INV-NNN — directly satisfies INV-020 through self-application and removes the 'implicit demonstration only' weakness from v1
Gate 1 updated to require INV-020 compliance: structural conformance gate now explicitly mandates verification prompt blocks in element spec chapters, closing the enforcement gap

Supporting infrastructure for ADR-011 and INV-020: §13.3 ADR Supersession Procedure added as a new operational section; §13.1 gains a DO NOT annotation citing ADR-011; Glossary gains 'ADR supersession' entry; Appendix C (Spec Error Taxonomy) gains two new error types ('Missing verification prompt', 'Superseded ADR without cascade'); anti-pattern catalog §8.3 gains 'The Missing Verification Prompt' with INV-020 reference
regressions_list: Namespace note in §0.13 still reads 'INV-001 through INV-019 and ADR-001 through ADR-010' — not updated to reflect INV-020 and ADR-011, creating a stale self-referential inaccuracy that violates the spec's own consistency principle (INV-015 analog for meta-standard self-description)
remaining_gaps: The §5.6 self-bootstrapping verification prompt (the explicit checklist for a DDIS-conforming spec) has only 6 checks and does not include an INV-020 check — the standard's own demonstration of verification prompts is not updated to cover its newest invariant
Namespace note in §0.13 is stale (INV-001 through INV-019, ADR-001 through ADR-010) — an LLM author reading the modularization section will learn the wrong invariant count for the meta-standard
Machine-readable cross-references (Open Question #1): no defined syntax for automated reference graph construction; cross-reference validation remains a manual O(sections²) audit
Multi-document specs (Open Question #2): cross-spec invariant referencing and ADR boundary conventions for systems with multiple DDIS specs remain unaddressed

No provisional ADR or confidence-level mechanism for early-stage specs (Open Question #5): ADR-011 addresses supersession of finalized decisions but not tentative decisions that are expected to change
rationale: v2 closes the primary self-conformance gap from v1 — verification prompt blocks are now both required (INV-020) and demonstrated (Chapters 2–7), and the ADR supersession problem is resolved via a locked protocol (ADR-011) with a concrete operational procedure (§13.3). Score improves from ~76 to ~82, well above the 3-point threshold, with 5 structural improvements and only 1 minor regression. Remaining gaps are all tractable: the stale namespace note, the incomplete §5.6 self-verification prompt, and three still-open questions from §0.10 that have never been addressed.
